program.name: Back in Shape
program.description: A 2-week general physical preparedness program designed to rebuild foundational strength, movement quality, and work capacity. Progressive loading from 70-80% 1RM across the cycle with balanced push/pull volume and varied energy system development.

movements.name: Back Squat
movements.notes: Week 1 baseline. Focus on depth and midline stability.
movements.scaling: Goblet squat 4x8 if 1RM unknown or returning from layoff

session_why.text: Week 1 Day 1 establishes baseline squat volume at moderate intensity (70% 1RM). Short-duration metcon (12 min AMRAP) targets glycolytic system without excessive systemic fatigue. Squat + hinge pattern metcon maintains movement synergy while avoiding posterior chain overload.

movement_why.text: Back squat chosen as primary lower body strength movement for Week 1 baseline. Metcon pairs hinge (KB swing), vertical pull (pull-up), and full-body conditioning (burpee) to balance squat-dominant strength work with posterior chain and upper body pulling volume.

loading_why.text: Straight sets at 70% 1RM for 5x5 structure builds baseline strength and work capacity. This intensity allows technical proficiency while accumulating sufficient volume for structural adaptation. 180-second rest ensures phosphocreatine recovery between sets.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

movements.name: Hang Power Clean
movements.notes: E2MOM x 8 rounds (16 min total). Complete 3 reps at top of each 2-min interval. Focus on hip extension and elbow turnover speed.
movements.scaling: Dumbbell hang clean or reduce to 60% 1RM if technique breaks down

session_why.text: Olympic lifting on Day 2 after 24h recovery from squats. EMOM format maintains session flow and auto-regulates rest. Medium metcon (8-12 min target) develops glycolytic capacity with monostructural rowing and power output (box jumps).

movement_why.text: Hang power clean targets explosive hip extension and receiving position without full squat demand, allowing recovery from Day 1 squats. Metcon pairs monostructural aerobic work (row) with lower body power (box jump) to develop mixed energy system capacity.

loading_why.text: EMOM strength format at 65% 1RM for 3 reps exploits auto-regulated rest while building technical proficiency. E2MOM interval provides adequate phosphocreatine recovery for power movements. Lower intensity than squat work allows CNS recovery while maintaining training stimulus.

movements.name: Bench Press
movements.notes: Ramp/build-up sets. Set 1: 5 reps @ 65%, Set 2: 4 reps @ 70%, Set 3: 3 reps @ 75%, Set 4: 2 reps @ 78%. Progressive neural preparation for horizontal push pattern.
movements.scaling: Dumbbell bench press with same progression structure

movements.name: Push-Up
movements.scaling: Knee push-up or reduce reps to 8

session_why.text: Upper body focus after rest day allows lower body recovery. Short metcon (10 min) maintains glycolytic stimulus without excessive fatigue accumulation. Push/pull pairing in both strength and metcon ensures balanced horizontal and vertical plane development.

movement_why.text: Bench press (horizontal push) paired with chest-to-bar pull-up metcon (vertical pull) creates balanced upper body volume. Double-unders add monostructural cardio component while maintaining upper body engagement through coordination demand.

loading_why.text: Ramp/build-up progression (5@65%, 4@70%, 3@75%, 2@78%) provides progressive neural preparation while developing technique under increasing load. This scheme appropriate for Week 1 baseline establishment and allows athletes to find working weights safely.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

movements.name: Front Squat
movements.notes: Week 1 baseline. Emphasize upright torso and elbow position.
movements.scaling: Goblet squat 4x8 if front rack mobility limited

session_why.text: Second squat session of Week 1 uses front squat variation to target quad-dominant pattern while reducing axial load compared to back squat. Medium metcon (12-14 min target) develops glycolytic capacity. Day 5 placement allows 48h recovery before Week 2 intensity increase.

movement_why.text: Front squat complements Day 1 back squat, providing squat volume without posterior chain overload. Metcon combines core demand (toes-to-bar), hinge pattern (KB swing), and conditioning (burpee) to develop work capacity across multiple planes.

loading_why.text: Straight sets at 72% 1RM (slightly higher than Day 1 back squat) accumulates volume for hypertrophy while maintaining technical quality. Front squat typically loaded at lower percentage than back squat due to greater demand on trunk stabilizers and mobility.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

strength.label: Gymnastics Skill — Pull-Up Progression + Body Tension

movements.name: Hollow Body Hold
movements.notes: Foundational body tension work. Maintain lower back contact with floor.

movements.name: Arch Body Hold
movements.notes: Complement to hollow position. Focus on glute engagement.

movements.name: Pull-Up (Strict)
movements.notes: Quality focus. Full range of motion, controlled descent.
movements.scaling: Banded pull-up or jumping pull-up with slow 3-sec negative

session_why.text: Day 6 gymnastics skill session develops pulling strength and body tension without high CNS demand. Pure aerobic monostructural work (2k row) satisfies weekly aerobic minimum and provides active recovery before rest day. Low interference with strength adaptations.
session_why.source: Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis

movement_why.text: Hollow and arch body holds are prerequisite foundational tension for all gymnastics movements. Strict pull-up development builds pulling strength capacity required before kipping progression. Rowing provides low-impact aerobic stimulus.

loading_why.text: Skill work uses moderate volume (4-5 sets) with full rest to prioritize movement quality over fatigue accumulation. Hold durations (20-30 sec) build structural endurance. Pull-up prescription focuses on quality reps (3-5) to develop strict strength foundation.

movements.name: Back Squat
movements.notes: Wave loading: Wave 1 (75%, 78%, 82%), Wave 2 (75%, 80%, 84%). Exploits post-activation potentiation to achieve higher loads in second wave.
movements.scaling: Reduce to straight sets 4x3 @ 77% if wave loading too complex

session_why.text: Week 2 increases intensity to 75-84% range via wave loading structure. Day 1 back squat builds on Week 1 baseline. Short EMOM metcon maintains glycolytic stimulus while allowing managed recovery between sets. 48h separation from Week 1 Day 5 front squat ensures adequate recovery.

movement_why.text: Back squat returns as primary lower body movement with increased complexity (wave loading). Metcon pairs lower body power (box jump) with vertical pull to balance squat-dominant strength work without posterior chain overload.

loading_why.text: Wave loading oscillates between heavier and lighter loads (75-78-82%, then 75-80-84%) to exploit post-activation potentiation. Heavy sets prime nervous system for subsequent performance, allowing higher loads in second wave while managing fatigue accumulation.

movements.name: Power Clean
movements.notes: Ramp/build-up to heavy single. Set 1-2: 3 reps, Set 3-4: 2 reps, Set 5: 1 rep. Focus on explosive hip extension throughout.
movements.scaling: Hang power clean with same structure or reduce percentages by 5%

session_why.text: Olympic lifting Day 2 with 24h separation from squats. Power clean (full movement vs Week 1 hang variation) increases complexity. Medium AMRAP develops mixed energy system capacity. Placement allows CNS recovery before rest day.

movement_why.text: Power clean progression from Week 1 hang variation. Full movement requires greater technical proficiency and posterior chain engagement. Metcon balances monostructural (bike), power (burpee box jump), and core (toes-to-bar) for comprehensive conditioning.

loading_why.text: Ramp/build-up structure (3-3-2-2-1 at 70-75-78-82-85%) provides progressive neural preparation while building to Week 2 intensity peak. Decreasing reps as load increases allows technique maintenance. This scheme appropriate for complex Olympic movements requiring high technical precision.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

movements.name: Strict Press (Overhead)
movements.notes: Top set + back-off volume. Set 1: Heavy double @ 85% for neural stimulus. Sets 2-5: Volume work @ 70% for structural adaptation.

movements.name: Ring Dip
movements.scaling: Box dip or parallel bar dip

movements.name: Handstand Push-Up
movements.scaling: Pike push-up or box pike push-up

session_why.text: Upper body day after rest allows lower body recovery. Strict press targets vertical push strength. Medium metcon (11-13 min target) develops glycolytic capacity with upper body emphasis (ring dip, HSPU) balanced by monostructural rowing.

movement_why.text: Strict press (vertical push) complements Week 1 Day 4 bench press (horizontal push). Metcon maintains push emphasis (ring dip, HSPU) with rowing providing aerobic component. Push-dominant volume balanced by pull work in subsequent sessions.

loading_why.text: Top set + back-off structure (1x2@85%, 4x5@70%) combines neural stimulation from heavy double with hypertrophy volume from back-off sets. This concurrent training approach maximizes adaptation diversity while managing CNS fatigue. Appropriate for Week 2 intensity build.

movements.name: Deadlift
movements.notes: E2MOM x 8 rounds (16 min total). Complete 3 reps at top of each 2-min interval. Focus on hip hinge mechanics and neutral spine.
movements.scaling: Trap bar deadlift or reduce to 75% 1RM

movements.name: Push-Up
movements.scaling: Knee push-up or reduce reps to 10

session_why.text: Deadlift provides hinge pattern volume without excessive posterior chain fatigue (48h before Week 2 end). EMOM format manages session flow. Short metcon maintains glycolytic stimulus. Day 5 placement allows recovery window before final gymnastics session.

movement_why.text: Deadlift fulfills hinge pattern requirement for Week 2. Placement after 48h from Day 1 squats respects posterior chain recovery needs. Metcon balances pull (pull-up), hinge (KB swing), and push (push-up) across all three fundamental patterns.

loading_why.text: E2MOM at 78% 1RM for 3 reps provides adequate rest for heavy hinge pattern while maintaining session efficiency. Week 2 intensity (78% vs Week 1 65-75% range) represents appropriate progressive overload. Interval ensures phosphocreatine recovery between sets.

strength.label: Gymnastics Skill — Ring Work + Body Tension

movements.name: Hollow Body Hold
movements.notes: Week 2 progression: increased duration from Week 1. Maintain quality over duration.
movements.scaling: Bent knee hollow hold or reduce to 20 sec

movements.name: Arch Body Hold
movements.notes: Week 2 progression. Focus on posterior chain engagement.

movements.name: Ring Dip
movements.notes: Quality focus. Full range of motion, stable rings at top and bottom.
movements.scaling: Box dip or parallel bar dip

session_why.text: Final session of 2-week program emphasizes gymnastics skill progression (ring dip vs Week 1 pull-up focus) and aerobic capacity. Low CNS demand allows recovery while maintaining training stimulus. AMRAP format with longer duration (15 min) develops oxidative capacity.

movement_why.text: Ring dip progression from Week 1 pull-up focus provides balanced push development. Hollow/arch holds continue foundational tension work with increased duration. Aerobic metcon uses pulling (row), monostructural (ski), and bodyweight (squat) for low-impact volume.

loading_why.text: Body tension holds progress to 25-30 sec (from Week 1 20-30 sec) for progressive overload. Ring dip volume (5x5-8) builds pushing strength and ring stability. Skill work maintains quality-over-quantity emphasis with full rest periods between sets.
